description: Seat expansion — Mid-Market at utilization ceiling

expected_pipeline_behavior.gate_action: Proceed automatically — Developing accounts are eligible for expansion analysis

expected_evidence.dau_wau_trend: Growing — DAU/WAU up 8 points over 30-day window
expected_evidence.documented_requests: CSM notes from 2026-03-14: champion (Dana Foss) mentioned two team members waiting on license access
expected_evidence.value_delivered: Documented QBR outcome: 23% reduction in report turnaround time (2026-02-01 QBR notes)

expected_output_sections: Adoption Health Check: Coverage 74 (Developing) — gate passed
Whitespace: Seats — Strong signal (88% utilization, documented access requests, growing DAU/WAU)
Business Case: Value Delivered to Date anchored on turnaround time reduction [Documented]
AE Briefing Message: internal, ≤200 words, no pricing language
CSM Next Actions: AE briefing as Action #1
notes: CSM hint matches evidence — no conflict flag. Timing note: 94 days to renewal, within optimal 90-120 day window.

description: Tier upgrade — Enterprise with documented feature requests

documented_feature_requests: QBR 2026-01-15: champion (Sandra Wu) requested SSO/SAML — listed as Enterprise Plus gated feature
CSM notes 2026-02-28: Sandra asked about advanced analytics dashboards — Enterprise Plus gated
CSM notes 2026-04-03: Sandra escalated SSO request to IT sponsor (David Reyes)
expected_evidence.timing: 120 days to renewal — optimal window for tier upgrade conversation
expected_evidence.exec_sponsor: David Reyes (IT Director) documented in CRM — engaged on SSO request

expected_output_sections: Adoption Health Check: Coverage 82 (Healthy) — gate passed
Whitespace: Tier Upgrade — Strong signal (3 documented Enterprise Plus feature requests in 90 days)
Business Case: Expansion narrative framed around customer-stated SSO and analytics goals [Documented]
Stakeholder Map: Sandra Wu (Champion), David Reyes (Exec Sponsor / IT), Caitlin Marsh (AE)
Timing Recommendation: Optimal — 120 days pre-renewal, post-SSO escalation provides natural entry point
notes: Three documented requests for the same tier-gated capability set qualifies as Strong signal. Executive already engaged on the request — stakeholder map should reflect active IT sponsor involvement.

description: Expansion hold — SMB account At Risk, gate triggers

expected_pipeline_behavior.gate_action: Pipeline paused — CSM override required before business case construction
expected_pipeline_behavior.override_rationale: Champion confirmed in 2026-05-10 call that adoption drop is due to Q1 internal project freeze; normal usage expected to resume by 2026-06-01. AE has a renewal call scheduled 2026-06-15.
expected_pipeline_behavior.whitespace_after_override: Seats — Moderate signal (utilization at 78% despite reduced activity period)

expected_pipeline_behavior_without_overr.output: Health gate warning surfaced with coverage score, status, and prompt for CSM to provide override rationale or redirect to adoption-motion

expected_output_with_override: Adoption Health Check: Coverage 43 (At Risk) — gate triggered; CSM override documented
Override Rationale: [CSM-provided text logged in report]
Whitespace: Seats — Moderate signal (utilization 78%; growth signals suppressed by project freeze)
Business Case: Confidence = Moderate due to At Risk health status and suppressed usage period
CSM Next Actions: Action #1 — brief AE on context before expansion conversation; note adoption recovery dependency
notes: At Risk accounts that receive an override should have the lower confidence reflected throughout the business case. The expansion pipeline proceeds but doesn't pretend the health flag didn't exist.

description: Adjacent team expansion — champion mentions peer department

expected_evidence.champion_reference: CSM notes 2026-04-22: champion (Roberto Alves) said 'our operations team has been asking about a tool like this'
expected_evidence.crm_contacts: CRM shows 2 contacts in Operations dept at Lakewood Financial (Samira Patel — Ops Manager, logged 2025-11-10; Derek Cho — Ops Analyst, logged 2026-01-08)
expected_evidence.core_coverage: 77% — strong core adoption by current team supports replication case

expected_output_sections: Whitespace: Adjacent Team — Strong signal (champion named ops team explicitly; 2 CRM ops contacts already logged)
Business Case: Expansion narrative framed as outcome replication — current team results applied to ops team workflow
Confidence: Moderate — Adjacent team interest is documented but ops team has not engaged directly
Stakeholder Map: Roberto Alves (Champion), Samira Patel (Ops Manager — potential expansion champion), Hannah Ruiz (AE)
CSM Next Actions: Confirm with Roberto whether Samira Patel is the right intro contact before AE outreach
notes: Two CRM contacts in the adjacent team elevates signal from Moderate to Strong per whitespace-analyzer thresholds. Business case confidence stays Moderate because the ops team has not directly expressed interest — it's champion-referenced, not ops-documented.

description: Expansion type hint conflict — CSM flags module but evidence supports tier upgrade

expected_pipeline_behavior.expansion_type_hint_result: CONFLICT — No integration whitespace found; evidence supports Tier Upgrade instead

expected_evidence.integration_whitespace: None found — Advanced Integrations Module features show no documented requests in cs-platform or CRM
tier_upgrade_signals: CSM notes 2026-03-11: champion (Chris Vance) asked about advanced analytics reporting — Enterprise Plus gated
CRM opportunity note 2026-04-19 (AE Brian Salazar): 'Chris mentioned wanting bulk export and API access — these are Plus features'
expected_evidence.seat_utilization: 91% — secondary seat whitespace signal also present

expected_conflict_handling.conflict_message: EXPANSION TYPE CONFLICT: CSM flagged 'module' (Advanced Integrations Module) but no integration whitespace was found in cs-platform or CRM records. Evidence supports Tier Upgrade (Strong signal: 2 documented Enterprise Plus feature requests). Business case built on evidence-supported type. CSM may override to module if additional context exists.

notes: The whitespace-analyzer should surface the conflict without silently overriding the CSM hint. The orchestrator builds the evidence-supported case and presents the conflict clearly, allowing the CSM to confirm or override. Seat whitespace (91% utilization) is noted as a secondary opportunity.
